title: The blocking constraints carry content matchers: the gate denies actual violations, not every edit in scope
context: Shipped from the roadmap. The scope-only forms of con_2ce3f2a547 and con_8460b6770f denied EVERY agent edit under src/synthesis/** and src/integrations/** at strict firmness — and, via blast radius, most of src/wiki/** too — forcing invariant-preserving work outside the tools.
decision: con_2ce3f2a547 forbids: dep @anthropic-ai/sdk + patterns api\\.anthropic\\.com / new Anthropic\\( (re-introducing pay-per-token API usage). con_8460b6770f forbids: patterns writeFileSync\\s*\\( / \\bwriteFile\\s*\\( (an added RAW write in the config-writer scope). Content-matched constraints also stop firing via blast radius (hookpolicy already skips them there). Updated in BOTH stores — the private overlay's copies win recs() id-dedupe, so a public-only edit would have silently not taken effect. Proven e2e: harmless edits to provider.ts/wiki.ts now inject grounding; re-adding the SDK import denies; adding a raw writeFileSync in integrations denies.
alternatives_rejected: Lowering firmness or severity: weakens the gate for humans and agents alike; precision, not permissiveness
Matching removal of the env-strip lines: added-line matchers cannot see deletions — that residual is covered by review + the conformance direction
Leaving the overlay copies unmatched: recs() serves the overlay record, so the fix would never reach the hook on overlay-configured machines
